folio.label: 17r
folio.l: Al be she , mayde , wydewe , or ellis wyff´
And eke it is nou©t likly , al thy lif´
To stonde in hire grace , no more shal I
For wel æè wost , thy seluen verayly
That æè and I , be dampned to prison
Perpetuelly , vs geyneth no raunsouö
We striue , as dide æe houndes , for æe bon
That faught al day , and ©et here ¹t was non
There cam a kyte , whyle æei were so wrothe

note.notetxt: 322ink darker probably fresh ink in the pen.ODR

folio.l: And bar a wey æe bon , bytwen hem bothe
And æäfore , at æe kynges court , my brother
Eche for hi~ self , there is non other
¢ Loue if æè list , for I loue and ay shal
And sothly leue brother , this is al
Here in prison , mote we endure
And ech of vs , take his auenture
¢ Gret was æe strif , and longe bytwen hem twey
If æat I had leyser , for to sey

note.notetxt: sey330same s as in GP double stroke, but it does not seem to refer to a capital letter.ODR

folio.l: But to the effect , it happed on a day
To telle it ©ow , as shortly as I may
A worthy Duke , that hight Parotheus
That felawe was , to æis Duke Theseus
Syn thilk day , æ æei were children lyte
Was come to Athenes , his felawe to visite
For to pley , as he was wont to do
For in the world , he loued no man so
And he loued hi~ , as tenderly a geyn
So wele they loued , as olde bokes seyn
That wha~ne æ on was ded , soth to telle
His felawe went , & sought hi~ doun in helle
But of that story , list me nat to wryte
Duke Parotheus , loued wel Arcite
And had hi~ knowe , at Thebes ©ere by ©ere
And finally at the request , and prayere
Of Parotheus , w oute ony raunsom
Duke Theseus , lete hi~ ou©t of prisou~
Freely to go , where as hi~ list ouä alle
In swich a gyse , as I ©ow telle shalle
This was the forward , pleynly to endite
By twene Theseus , and this Arcite
That if so were , æ Arcite were founde
